stages.title: Stage 1 · Sequencing context, kit, goal, and sample context
stages.summary: Start with what you are sequencing, then choose the library prep kit or run mode, then the main analytic goal, and finally the sample context that best matches the closest published example in this repository.

questions.label: What are you sequencing?
questions.description: Choose the broad sequencing context first. Repo-specific workflows appear later as published examples rather than as the first conceptual layer.

options.label: Environmental metagenomics
options.help: Environmental DNA or RNA community sequencing, including shotgun metagenomics, virome work, and 12S metabarcoding branches.

options.label: Clinical / isolate
options.help: Purified isolates, barcoded isolate collections, or clinical metagenomes linked to host-association analysis.

options.label: Food safety
options.help: Food-associated enrichment and Listeria-focused adaptive-sampling or control workflows.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Keep the first routing step neutral and let the later answers narrow the route.

questions.label: Which library prep kit or run mode did you use?
questions.description: The kit or run mode changes early preprocessing and often determines whether the route is single-sample, barcoded, amplicon-based, or adaptive-sampling oriented.

options.label: LSK114
options.help: Ligation-based ONT workflow for single-sample, high-yield DNA sequencing.

consequences.demultiplexing: Usually not required for single-sample ligation runs.
consequences.barcode_trimming: Barcode trimming is not usually expected.
consequences.typical_use: Single-sample runs with maximal read length and yield.
consequences.route_implication: In clinical / isolate contexts, this defaults toward a single-isolate assembly and AMR route.
consequences.first_steps: Basecall, trim adapters, then continue into QC and downstream analysis.

options.label: RBK114.24
options.help: Rapid Barcoding Kit 24 V14 for up to 24 DNA samples.

consequences.barcode_trimming: Barcode trimming is expected after demultiplexing.
consequences.typical_use: Rapid multiplexed DNA sequencing with fast turnaround.
consequences.route_implication: In clinical / isolate contexts, this defaults toward a barcoded-isolate route rather than a single-sample isolate route.
consequences.first_steps: Basecall, demultiplex, trim barcodes and adapters, then run QC and filtering.

options.label: NBD114.24
options.help: Native Barcoding Kit 24 V14 for multiplexed ligation-based DNA libraries.

consequences.barcode_trimming: Barcode trimming is expected after demultiplexing.
consequences.typical_use: Multiplexed ligation workflow that preserves fragment length better than rapid barcoding.
consequences.route_implication: In clinical / isolate contexts, this also defaults toward a barcoded-isolate route.
consequences.first_steps: Basecall, demultiplex, trim barcodes and adapters, then proceed to QC and downstream analysis.

options.label: Amplicon workflow
options.help: Amplicon-oriented ONT workflow used here for eDNA 12S metabarcoding branches.

consequences.demultiplexing: Demultiplexing depends on whether barcoded amplicon pools were used.
consequences.barcode_trimming: Barcode or primer trimming is branch-specific.
consequences.typical_use: Targeted 12S amplicons rather than shotgun long-read metagenomics.
consequences.route_implication: Routes environmental cases toward the published eDNA 12S examples.
consequences.first_steps: Start from basecalled amplicon reads, then follow branch-specific trimming and classification steps.

options.label: Barcoded metagenome
options.help: Multiplexed metagenomic sequencing rather than purified isolate sequencing.

consequences.barcode_trimming: Barcode trimming is expected if pooled libraries are still combined.
consequences.typical_use: Clinical metagenomes or pooled complex samples rather than isolate assemblies.
consequences.route_implication: Routes clinical metagenome cases toward the AMR host-association example.
consequences.first_steps: Basecall, demultiplex if needed, then continue to contig-level host-association analysis.

options.label: Adaptive sampling
options.help: Real-time ONT enrichment or depletion during sequencing.

consequences.demultiplexing: Demultiplexing depends on how the adaptive-sampling run was configured.
consequences.barcode_trimming: Barcode trimming depends on the paired library design.
consequences.typical_use: Target recovery or enrichment comparisons during sequencing.
consequences.route_implication: Routes food safety cases toward the published Listeria adaptive-sampling example.
consequences.first_steps: Preserve the adaptive-sampling run outputs, then compare target recovery against the matched control branch.

options.label: Standard sequencing control
options.help: Matched non-adaptive sequencing control for food-safety comparisons.

consequences.demultiplexing: Demultiplexing depends on the control-library design.
consequences.typical_use: Control arm for adaptive-sampling comparisons and target recovery benchmarking.
consequences.route_implication: Keeps food safety cases inside the published Listeria comparison framework without implying broader food-safety coverage.
consequences.first_steps: Keep control BAM or FASTQ outputs paired with the adaptive-sampling arm for direct comparison.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Keep kit selection neutral and refine the route with goal and sample context.

questions.label: What do you want to do with the data?
questions.description: Choose one main analytic goal. Use the full-pipeline option when you want the broadest documented action sheet for that route.

options.label: Taxonomy profiling
options.help: Determine which organisms are present in the sample.

options.label: Functional profiling
options.help: Profile genes, pathways, and functional potential from environmental long reads.

options.label: MAG recovery
options.help: Assemble and recover metagenome-assembled genomes.

options.label: AMR surveillance
options.help: Screen environmental sequencing data for pathogen and resistance signals.

options.label: RNA virome
options.help: Characterize untargeted RNA viral diversity from environmental samples.

options.label: Full environmental pipeline
options.help: Receive the broadest action sheet for environmental long-read DNA workflows.

options.label: eDNA 12S biodiversity
options.help: Amplicon-based 12S metabarcoding for biodiversity and host-range inference.

options.label: Full amplicon pipeline
options.help: Receive the broadest 12S metabarcoding action sheet.

options.label: Complete genome assembly
options.help: Aim for near-complete or closed isolate genomes.

options.label: Annotation
options.help: Genome annotation and downstream isolate interpretation.

options.label: Hybrid assembly
options.help: Combine ONT and short-read data for isolate assembly refinement.

options.label: AMR / virulence profiling
options.help: Profile antimicrobial resistance and virulence determinants.

options.label: Full isolate pipeline
options.help: Receive the broadest action sheet for the current isolate route.

options.label: Genome assembly + annotation
options.help: Per-isolate assembly and annotation from barcoded isolate runs.

options.label: Phylogenetics / outbreak tracking
options.help: Comparative isolate analysis after per-barcode assembly.

options.label: Plasmid reconstruction
options.help: Resolve plasmids and related isolate structures from barcoded runs.

options.label: AMR host association
options.help: Associate AMR genes or plasmids with hosts in clinical metagenomes.

options.label: Full host-association pipeline
options.help: Receive the broadest action sheet for the AMR host-association route.

options.label: Listeria target recovery
options.help: Recover and analyze Listeria targets from food-associated samples.

options.label: AS vs native comparison
options.help: Compare adaptive sampling against the matched non-adaptive control.

options.label: Recovered target AMR profiling
options.help: Profile AMR features after target recovery from food-associated sequencing.

options.label: Full food safety pipeline
options.help: Receive the broadest action sheet for the published food-safety route.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Keep the analytic goal neutral and let the route map to the nearest published example.

questions.label: Which sample context best matches your data?
questions.description: This is where the selector maps a generalized route to the closest published example backend already present in the repository.

options.label: Air / bioaerosol
options.help: Low-biomass air or impinger-derived samples.

options.label: Wetland or passive water sample
options.help: Passive water or wetland surveillance samples in the current published system.

options.label: Water eDNA sample
options.help: Water-based eDNA metabarcoding outside the wetland passive-sampling branch.

options.label: Soil
options.help: Used to trigger honest nearest-example guidance because no soil workflow is published here.

options.label: Other environmental sample
options.help: Environmental matrix outside the published air, wetland, or water-eDNA examples.

options.label: Bacterial isolate
options.help: Purified isolate for assembly, typing, plasmid work, or AMR profiling.

options.label: Clinical metagenome
options.help: Complex clinical material rather than a purified isolate.

options.label: Other isolate
options.help: Isolate context that is not exactly the published bacterial-isolate examples.

options.label: Food safety sample
options.help: Food-associated enrichment or target-recovery material.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Keep sample context neutral.

stages.title: Stage 2 · Setup and entry point
stages.summary: These setup questions refine the action sheet. They do not decide the biological route by themselves.

questions.label: Current input format
questions.description: Select the file type you already have in hand.

options.label: POD5
options.help: Raw current-signal input in POD5 format.

options.label: FAST5
options.help: Legacy raw current-signal input.

options.label: Basecalled BAM
options.help: Basecalled BAM or sequencing-aligned BAM.

options.label: FASTQ
options.help: Basecalled reads in FASTQ format.

options.label: Paired-end FASTQ
options.help: Typical for short-read amplicon or hybrid-assembly support data.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Keep input format neutral.

questions.label: Are your data multiplexed?
questions.description: This refines entry-point wording and kit consequences. It does not choose the route on its own.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Barcoded pooled samples still need per-sample handling.

options.label: No
options.help: Single-sample data or already separated outputs.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Keep multiplexing neutral.

questions.label: Demultiplexing status
questions.description: Use this to clarify whether the first steps should still separate pooled reads.

options.label: Still needed
options.help: The reads are not yet separated by barcode or sample.

options.label: Already done
options.help: Per-sample or per-barcode outputs already exist.

options.label: Not needed
options.help: Single-sample route or already unpooled data.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Keep demultiplexing neutral.

questions.label: Basecalling state or preferred Dorado tier
questions.description: Refines ONT setup guidance and the documented entry point.

options.label: Raw data, not basecalled yet
options.help: You still need a basecalling step.

options.label: Prefer FAST
options.help: Fastest Dorado tier with the lowest compute cost.

options.label: Prefer HAC
options.help: Balanced accuracy and compute cost.

options.label: Prefer SUP
options.help: Accuracy-first Dorado tier with the highest compute demand.

options.label: Already basecalled
options.help: Reads are already available in FASTQ or BAM.

options.label: Not applicable
options.help: Use this when the selected entry point no longer depends on basecalling.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Keep basecalling state neutral.

questions.label: Adapter trimming and read filtering
questions.description: Use this to refine whether the action sheet should begin before or after trimming and filtering.

options.label: Need trimming and filtering
options.help: Adapter trimming and read filtering are still pending.

options.label: Need trimming only
options.help: Adapters still need to be removed.

options.label: Need filtering only
options.help: Reads are trimmed but still need quality or length filtering.

options.label: Already trimmed and filtered
options.help: You are entering after read cleaning.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Keep preprocessing state neutral.
